name: Newfoundland
description: Developed by fishermen on the island of Newfoundland as a working water dog capable of hauling nets and rescuing drowning sailors in icy Atlantic waters, the Newfoundland has webbed feet and a water-resistant double coat perfectly adapted for cold-water swimming. Famously patient and gentle with children, J.M. Barrie immortalized the breed as Nana the nursemaid dog in Peter Pan, a portrayal that resonates with Newf owners who know the breed's natural watchfulness over children. Their enormous size makes early obedience training essential before they become too powerful to manage safely on a leash.

conditions_detail.name: Hip Dysplasia
conditions_detail.description: Hip dysplasia is a genetic condition where the hip joint doesn't develop properly, causing the ball and socket to not fit together correctly. This leads to joint deterioration, pain, and arthritis over time.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Treatment ranges from weight management and physical therapy for mild cases to total hip replacement for severe cases. NSAIDs and joint supplements help manage pain.
prevention_tips: Screen breeding stock with hip scoring

conditions_detail.name: Elbow Dysplasia
conditions_detail.description: Elbow dysplasia is an umbrella term covering several developmental abnormalities of the elbow joint including fragmented coronoid process, osteochondritis dissecans, and ununited anconeal process. It causes foreleg lameness and arthritis.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Surgical intervention is often recommended to remove bone fragments or reshape joint surfaces. Medical management includes NSAIDs, joint supplements, and physiotherapy.
prevention_tips: Control growth rate in large breeds

conditions_detail.name: Cystinuria
conditions_detail.description: Cystinuria is an inherited defect in renal tubular transport of cystine and related amino acids, leading to cystine crystal and stone formation in the urinary tract. It predominantly affects males, who are more prone to urethral obstruction.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Dissolution with D-penicillamine, tiopronin, or dietary management (low-protein, alkalinising diets) can dissolve some cystine stones. Surgical cystotomy for stones that cannot be dissolved. Lifelong prevention diet is needed.
prevention_tips: DNA test available for Newfoundland and Labrador forms
High water intake and alkalinising diet
